ProjectsPage.title: Systems & Products
ProjectsPage.description: End-to-end architected digital products, from full-stack development to data automation solutions.

bex.title: BEX — Groq-Powered AI Assistant
bex.tagline: Real-time streaming AI, live in production.
bex.description: Real-time AI assistant running on Groq LLM (Llama 3.1 8B), integrated into the portfolio. Features streaming responses, multilingual UI, and a PDF analysis module.
bex.challenge: Building a real-time AI assistant that feels instant, handles multiple languages, and integrates document analysis — all within a single portfolio page with no separate backend.
bex.solution: Chose Groq's inference API for sub-second latency, implemented streaming via Vercel Edge Functions, and built a session-aware chat UI with PDF upload handled client-side before vectorization.
bex.outcome: Consistently < 300ms first-token latency. Handles 7 languages. PDF analysis on documents up to 10MB. Zero cold-start overhead via Edge Runtime.
bex.tech_decision: Groq over OpenAI for 10x lower latency on the Llama 3.1 8B model. Edge Runtime over Node.js for global distribution without cold starts.

portfolio.title: Multilingual Portfolio Infrastructure
portfolio.description: Production-ready portfolio system with Next.js 16 App Router, 7-language support via next-intl, Supabase backend, n8n automation webhooks, and Vercel Edge deployment.
portfolio.challenge: Designing a portfolio that serves as both a personal brand and a live technical demonstration — i18n, performance, SEO, and real backend integrations all simultaneously.
portfolio.solution: Next.js App Router with server components for SEO and RSC for data. next-intl for locale-aware routing and fallback strategy. n8n webhooks for contact form handling without a dedicated backend server.
portfolio.outcome: Lighthouse score > 95 across all metrics. 7 locales with automatic fallback. 68 static pages generated at build time. Contact form goes through n8n webhook pipeline.
portfolio.tech_decision: next-intl over i18next for better RSC/App Router support. Static generation over SSR for most pages to minimize TTFB globally.

booking.title: Intelligent Booking System with n8n
booking.description: Serverless appointment automation syncing Google Calendar, SMS webhooks, and email notifications. Zero manual intervention via n8n workflows.
booking.challenge: A barber shop needed automated appointment management: customer-facing booking form, real-time calendar sync, SMS confirmation, and daily summary reports — without a custom backend.
booking.solution: Built an n8n workflow connecting the booking form webhook → Google Calendar availability check → slot reservation → SMS via Twilio → email confirmation. All serverless.
booking.outcome: Eliminated 2+ hours/day of manual scheduling. 0 double-bookings since deployment. SMS confirmation rate > 98%. Setup took 3 days.
booking.tech_decision: n8n over custom Node.js because visual workflows are faster to iterate and non-developers on the client side can modify logic without touching code.

saas.title: Full-Stack SaaS Boilerplate
saas.tagline: Ship your SaaS in days, not months.
saas.description: Production-ready SaaS template with Auth (Supabase Auth), subscription management (Stripe), multi-tenant architecture, role-based access control, and i18n.
saas.challenge: Every new SaaS project starts with the same 3–4 weeks of infrastructure work: auth, billing, permissions, environments. Building a reusable, opinionated boilerplate to skip this.
saas.solution: Supabase Auth with Row Level Security for multi-tenancy. Stripe Checkout + webhooks for subscription lifecycle. RBAC middleware on Next.js API routes. Fully typed end-to-end with Zod schemas.
saas.outcome: Reduces SaaS bootstrap time from ~4 weeks to < 2 days. All auth flows tested. Stripe integration handles plan upgrades, downgrades, and cancellations automatically.
saas.tech_decision: Supabase over Firebase for PostgreSQL (proper relations, SQL, RLS). Stripe over Paddle for developer experience and webhook reliability.

pdf.title: Document Analysis Engine
pdf.tagline: Ask questions to any PDF. Get answers instantly.
pdf.description: Tool that uploads PDF documents and analyzes, summarizes, and answers questions using Groq LLM. Built on a RAG architecture foundation.
pdf.challenge: Allowing users to upload arbitrary PDF documents and query them via natural language — without a vector database or heavy infrastructure, running at the edge.
pdf.solution: Custom PDF parser using PDF.js that extracts text client-side. Chunking and in-memory context window management. Groq LLM for Q&A with streaming responses. No vector DB needed for documents under 50 pages.
pdf.outcome: Handles documents up to 10MB / ~50 pages. Average query response time < 500ms. Context-aware multi-turn conversations within a single document session.
pdf.tech_decision: No vector DB for this use case — context window management is sufficient for typical documents. Groq over local LLMs for deployment simplicity and latency.

flashcards.try_different_filter: Try a different level or category filter.

grammar.intro: Read each rule and mark complete to earn XP.

exam.title: Level Exam
exam.intro: Pick a mode and start a {count}-question exam. Each mode uses different question styles.
exam.exit_confirm: Leave the exam? Your progress will not be saved.

mode_desc.mixed: A mix of all question types.
mode_desc.true_false: Mark each statement true or false.
mode_desc.tap: Quickly tap the correct option from two choices.
mode_desc.fill: Type the missing word in the sentence.
mode_desc.translate: Translate the Turkish sentence into English.

taboo.title: Taboo Game
taboo.subtitle: Everyday words everyone knows. Describe in English without forbidden words — perfect for classroom team play!

modes.practice_desc: No timer, hints and pronunciation support.
modes.party_desc: Two teams take turns with a live scoreboard.

taboo.no_cards_filter: No cards match this filter. Try a different category or difficulty.

rules.title: Taboo Rules
rules.r1: Describe the target word to your team in English.
rules.r2: You cannot use forbidden words or their root forms.
rules.r3: You cannot say or spell the target word directly.
rules.r4: If you say a forbidden word, opponents call 'Taboo!' — turn passes.
rules.r5: In timed mode, time out auto-skips the card.
rules.r6: In team mode, turns alternate after each card.

heads_up.title: Heads Up
heads_up.subtitle: Hold the phone facing your friends. They describe the word in English — you guess.
heads_up.how_1: One person holds the phone and does not look at the screen.
heads_up.how_2: Friends see the word and describe it in English.
heads_up.how_3: Got it? Green · Pass? Yellow · Round ends when time runs out.
heads_up.friends_describe: Your friends are describing this word
heads_up.tilt_hint: Large buttons — tap with your thumb

charades.title: Charades
charades.subtitle: Only you see the word. Act without speaking — your team guesses in English!
charades.how_1: One player holds the phone and sees the word.
charades.how_2: Mime and gesture — no talking allowed.
charades.how_3: Team guessed right? Green · Pass? Yellow.
charades.only_you_see: Only you can see this word

category_blitz.title: Category Blitz
category_blitz.subtitle: Scattergories-style classroom game — get a letter and category, your team names English words.
category_blitz.how_1: A letter and category appear on screen (e.g. B — Hollywood Movies).
category_blitz.how_2: Within the time limit, say a valid English word starting with that letter.
category_blitz.how_3: The hint button shows example answers — use them to learn.
category_blitz.prompt: Name an English word starting with this letter!
category_blitz.hint_letter_note: You need a word starting with "{letter}" — the list above is for inspiration.

emoji_clues.title: Emoji Clues
emoji_clues.tagline: Combine the emojis → find one English word. (Guess the Emoji logic)
emoji_clues.rule_body: Read the emojis left to right. Each emoji is a clue. Together they point to a single English word. The Turkish meaning appears only after you answer.

picture.title: Picture Mode
picture.prompt: What word do these 2 emojis describe?
picture.hint: Tip: The emojis depict the thing itself.

combo.title: Combo Mode
combo.prompt: What word do these 3 emojis make together?
combo.hint: Tip: Think about each emoji in order.

chain.title: Story Mode
chain.prompt: What English word does this scene describe?
chain.hint: Tip: The emojis tell a situation or activity.

ServicesPage.title: Engineering services for founders and teams.
ServicesPage.subtitle: I work with startups and technical founders who need production-grade systems built fast. Three focused service areas, one engineer who ships.
ServicesPage.service_1_desc: From zero to production. I architect and build full-stack applications using Next.js, Supabase, and modern deployment pipelines. Auth, payments, APIs, database — complete.
ServicesPage.service_2_desc: Connecting LLMs and automation to your product or workflow. I build AI-powered features and n8n workflow automations that eliminate manual processes.
ServicesPage.service_3_desc: Architecture review, bottleneck analysis, and tech stack decisions. For teams that want a senior engineer's perspective without full-time commitment.
ServicesPage.process_step_1_desc: 30-minute call to understand your problem, timeline, and goals.
ServicesPage.process_step_2_desc: Clear scope, deliverables, timeline, and fixed or hourly pricing.
ServicesPage.process_step_3_desc: Weekly check-ins, async updates, working demos at each milestone.
ServicesPage.process_step_4_desc: Clean handoff with documentation, deployment, and 2 weeks of post-launch support.
ServicesPage.faq_1_q: Do you work on long-term retainers?
ServicesPage.faq_1_a: Yes. For ongoing development or consulting, I offer monthly retainer arrangements. Minimum 3 months.
ServicesPage.faq_2_a: MVPs typically take 4–8 weeks. AI integrations 2–4 weeks. Consulting engagements are flexible.
ServicesPage.faq_3_q: Do you work with non-technical founders?
ServicesPage.faq_3_a: Absolutely. I translate business requirements into technical decisions and keep you informed without jargon.
ServicesPage.faq_4_q: What technologies do you not work with?
ServicesPage.faq_4_a: My stack is Next.js, Supabase, and Node.js-based. I don't take mobile (native iOS/Android) or blockchain projects.
ServicesPage.cta_desc: Send a message and we'll figure out if there's a fit.

ResumePage.title: Berkay Türkyılmaz
ResumePage.subtitle: Frontend Developer
ResumePage.summary: Frontend Developer focused on high-performance, user-oriented web interfaces for large-scale corporate environments such as Türk Telekom. Strong foundation in modern JavaScript, HTML5, and CSS3/SASS. Builds scalable interfaces, automation systems, and accessible products aligned with modern web standards. Experienced in Agile teams, coordinating with UX/UI teams, and cross-functional collaboration.
ResumePage.exp_1_desc: Develop frontend architecture for web applications used by customers and internal teams according to modern web standards.
ResumePage.exp_1_bullet_1: Translate Figma and Zeplin designs into fully responsive, cross-browser compatible code to improve user experience.
ResumePage.exp_1_bullet_2: Participate in Agile processes via Jira and coordinate with backend teams for data integration.
ResumePage.exp_1_bullet_3: Build scalable, accessible interfaces for high-traffic corporate web products.
ResumePage.exp_2_desc: Supported the creation of interactive web interfaces using HTML, CSS, and basic JavaScript.
ResumePage.exp_2_bullet_1: Assisted in building interactive UI components from design handoffs.
ResumePage.exp_2_bullet_2: Performed UI testing and helped fix identified bugs.
ResumePage.project_1_desc: Personal web project built with modern interface techniques and responsive design principles, deployed on Vercel. Includes AI Lab (BEX assistant, document analysis, EnglishPath).
ResumePage.project_2_desc: Designed automated n8n workflows to fetch, process, and report financial data from external APIs — reducing manual workload through logical data flows.

PrivacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
PrivacyPage.intro: This privacy policy explains how berkay-dev.vercel.app collects, uses, and protects information when you visit this site or use the contact form.
PrivacyPage.section_1_body: When you submit the contact form, the following data is collected: your name, email address, selected subject, and message content. No other personal data is collected automatically.
PrivacyPage.section_2_body: Contact form submissions are used solely to respond to your inquiry. Data is transmitted via a secure webhook to an n8n automation pipeline and is not stored in a third-party database.
PrivacyPage.section_3_body: This site does not use Google Analytics, cookies, or any third-party tracking scripts. There are no tracking pixels or behavioral analytics.
PrivacyPage.section_4_body: This site is deployed on Vercel, which may collect standard server logs (IP address, timestamp, request path) as part of their infrastructure. See Vercel's privacy policy for details.
PrivacyPage.section_5_body: Contact form data is retained only as long as necessary to respond to the inquiry. You may request deletion at any time by emailing berkaytrkylmzz@gmail.com.
PrivacyPage.section_6_body: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. To exercise these rights, contact berkaytrkylmzz@gmail.com.
PrivacyPage.section_7_body: For any privacy-related questions, email berkaytrkylmzz@gmail.com.

AboutPage.title: Frontend at scale. Side projects with modern web.
AboutPage.description: I'm a Frontend Developer building high-performance, user-oriented web interfaces at Türk Telekom (via One Bilgi Sistemleri). I work with JavaScript, HTML5, CSS3/SASS, and React — turning Figma/Zeplin designs into responsive, accessible products in Agile teams. On my own time I build this portfolio and automation experiments with Next.js, n8n, and AI integrations.
